issue_title: Torch.compile does not guard on requires_grad_ state - uses stale compilation
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `high priority`. Maintainer `high priority` label confirms the bug is important and acknowledged.

linked_prs.title: [dynamo][guards] Check requires_grad value on the fast path
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR is a ghstack PR (body begins with 'Stack from [ghstack]:'). It received an APPROVED review from MEMBER williamwen42 and CONTRIBUTOR Lucaskabela, and CONTRIBUTOR anijain2305 wrote '@pytorchbot merge' on 2026-03-12. pytorchmergebot started the merge process but the PR closed with merged=false — consistent with ghstack's branch-cleanup behavior after the stack tip is processed. The issue remains OPEN as of 2026-04-24 with no subsequent comment confirming the fix landed.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: MEMBER williamwen42 submitted APPROVED review on 2026-03-12. CONTRIBUTOR Lucaskabela submitted APPROVED review on 2026-03-12 after C++ nit fixes. CONTRIBUTOR anijain2305 (the author and primary investigator of the root cause) wrote '@pytorchbot merge' on 2026-03-12 and pytorchmergebot started the merge. PR closed with merged=false — likely a ghstack artifact where the individual stack PR was closed after the stack tip was processed. Root cause was confirmed: skip_tensor_guards_with_matching_dict_tags optimization treats parameters as immutable, so in-place requires_grad_() mutation bypasses guard evaluation.

maintainer_signals.author: williamwen42
maintainer_signals.quote: we are generating the seemingly correct TENSOR_MATCH guard: `TENSOR_MATCH: check_tensor(G['conv']._parameters['weight']...requires_grad=False...)` but we're not recompiling, so something is probably wrong with guard eval.

confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: MEMBER williamwen42 approved fix (PR #177158 APPROVED review) → +0.20. Running total: 0.95. Step 3: Repro script present — exact 20-line Python snippet in issue body demonstrating two backward passes both returning grad=None after requires_grad_(True) is set on a compiled module's weight → +0.15. Running total: 1.10. Step 4: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.20. Step 5: fix_scope is 'single_file' (torch/csrc/dynamo/guards.cpp) — PR #177158 modifies DictGetItemGuardAccessor and FrameLocalsGuardAccessor to snapshot and check requires_grad on the fast path → +0.10. Running total: 1.30. Step 6: '@pytorchbot merge' requested by PR author anijain2305 (CONTRIBUTOR who is the primary investigator of the root cause, familiar with guard system) → +0.05 partial credit (not COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER but strong engagement from guard expert). Running total: 1.35. Step 7: Issue is only ~7 months old, PT2-Bug-Bash label confirms active priority. No negative adjustments apply. Step 8: PR is a ghstack PR closed with merged=false after merge request — typical ghstack behavior. Issue still OPEN. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0]: 1.00. Final: apply -0.10 for ghstack uncertainty (fix may have partially landed or may still need to land): 0.90.
ground_truth_delta: Very similar to positive_71774 (silent correctness bug with an approved-but-unmerged fix PR), with several advantages: (1) Much more recent (2025/2026 vs 2022), so the codebase is far less likely to have diverged. (2) Root cause is definitively confirmed by MEMBER williamwen42 who identified the exact guard evaluation bypass in skip_tensor_guards_with_matching_dict_tags. (3) A specific fix in guards.cpp is understood and reviewed. Differences: (1) The merge requester (anijain2305) is CONTRIBUTOR not COLLABORATOR, making the @pytorchbot merge signal slightly weaker. (2) The fix involves changes to guard performance optimization infrastructure, which is more complex than addmm_cpu for int64. A contributor could revive PR #177158 or its code changes from guards.cpp.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #177158 ('[dynamo][guards] Check requires_grad value on the fast path') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.
contribution_path.warning: What killed this PR: PR is a ghstack PR (body begins with 'Stack from [ghstack]:'). It received an APPROVED review from MEMBER williamwen42 and CONTRIBUTOR Lucaskabela, and CONTRIBUTOR anijain2305 wrote '@pytorchbot merge

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #162174. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: PR #177158 is a ghstack PR with an APPROVED review from MEMBER williamwen42. Check whether it can be extracted from the stack and submitted independently. If the stack is stale, re-implement the guard fix that williamwen42 confirmed: the TENSOR_MATCH guard is generated correctly but not invalidated when requires_grad_ changes.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/dynamo/test_dynamo.py -k requires_grad`. Add a test showing that a cached compiled function is correctly recompiled when `requires_grad_` is called on an input tensor.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #162174' and 'Revives #177158' in the body. Tag @williamwen42 for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
contribution_path.warning: Check PR #177158 status first — if still open and under active review, contributing to it is better than opening a competing PR.
